question: Tell me about a time you faced a tight deadline and how you managed it.
Explain how channels work in Go and what happens when you write to a closed channel.
What is a goroutine in Go, and how does it differ from a traditional OS thread?
How do you handle errors in Go? Why doesn't Go use traditional try-catch blocks?
What is the difference between buffered and unbuffered channels in Go?
Explain how the 'defer' statement works in Go and give a practical use case.
What is a struct in Go, and how do you implement a method on a struct?
How do slices work under the hood in Go? What happens when a slice triggers an append beyond its capacity?
What is the purpose of the 'select' statement in Go concurrency?
Explain the difference between passing a variable by value vs. passing by pointer in Go.
What is an Interface in Go? How does implicit interface satisfaction work, and how does it differ from explicit implementation in other languages?
Explain the concept of 'context' in Go. Why is it standard practice to pass a 'ctx context.Context' as the first argument in backend functions?
How does Garbage Collection work in Go, and how does Go's escape analysis decide whether a variable goes to the Stack or the Heap?
What is Dependency Injection (DI) in Go? How do you typically structure your project to inject database clients or services without a heavy framework?
What are 'init()' functions in Go? When are they executed, and what is a common use case (or anti-pattern) for using them?
Explain how a 'sync.WaitGroup' works and how you can use it to block execution until multiple goroutines finish.
What is the difference between 'sync.Mutex' and 'sync.RWMutex' in Go? When should you prefer one over the other?
What is the difference between a Relational Database (SQL) and a Non-Relational Database (NoSQL)?
What are Database Indexes? How do they speed up read operations, and what is their impact on write operations?
Explain the difference between an INNER JOIN, a LEFT JOIN, and a RIGHT JOIN in SQL.
What are ACID properties in a database transaction? Can you explain each one briefly?
What is database normalization, and why do we design tables up to the Third Normal Form (3NF)?
What is a Foreign Key constraint, and what happens during a cascade delete operation?
What are the main HTTP methods used in a RESTful API, and what are their typical use cases?
What is the meaning of the HTTP status codes 200, 201, 400, 401, 403, 404, and 500?
What is the difference between Authentication and Authorization in web applications?
How does JWT (JSON Web Token) authentication work, and how does a backend verify a token securely?
What is the purpose of middleware in a backend web server framework?
What is CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing), and why does the browser block requests to different domains?
Explain the difference between monolithic architecture and microservices architecture.
What is Git, and what is the difference between 'git merge' and 'git rebase'?
What is a Docker container, and how does it differ from a standard Virtual Machine?
What is the purpose of a Dockerfile, and what do the commands FROM, RUN, and EXPOSE do?
How do you search for a specific text pattern inside a log file using the Linux command line?
What is a CI/CD pipeline, and why is it useful in modern software development workflows?
What is Object-Oriented Programming (OOP), and what are its four core principles?
What is Big O notation? Can you compare the time complexities of O(1), O(N), and O(N^2)?
What is the difference between synchronous execution and asynchronous execution?
What is a memory leak, and how can it occur in a backend environment?
What are the SOLID principles? Pick one and explain how it helps write cleaner code.
Why do you want to work as a Junior Back-end Developer, and what motivated you to learn backend programming?
Describe a difficult technical bug you encountered in a project and the steps you took to debug and solve it.
How do you handle constructive criticism or code review feedback that asks you to rewrite a large part of your code?
Tell me about a time you had to work closely with someone whose personality or working style was very different from yours.
What do you do when you are assigned a task using a technology or framework that you have never used before?
Imagine you push code to production and it immediately crashes the system. What are your first steps to handle the situation?
How do you prioritize your tasks when you have multiple assignments with similar priority levels?
Tell me about a project you are most proud of. What role did you play, and what did you learn from it?
Where do you see your technical skills expanding over the next two years? What areas do you want to master next?
Why is writing clear documentation or commenting code important, especially when working within a dev team?
